summary: Full backend pytest + frontend Playwright e2e of Coderift. Backend: 14/15 pytest pass (1 known test bug per E1: test_register asserts uppercase email but server lowercases — IGNORED). Frontend: 11/11 critical flows working — Landing, protected routes, register, dashboard, create room (8-char ID), Monaco editor + all toolbar/rail buttons, Run (stdout 'Hello, Coder!'), AI Review (1.8KB markdown from Gemini), AI Fix (apply-fix-btn visible), Login (alice@test.com). One minor UX issue: logout from /dashboard lands on /login instead of / (Protected route Navigate fires before navbar's nav('/')). Socket.IO handshake passes; one console warning about WebSocket transport closing during navigation (auto-falls back to polling, not blocking).

minor.issue: Test bug: asserts uppercase email back but server (correctly) lowercases. Per E1 instructions: ignore — server behaviour is correct.

ui_bugs.issue: After clicking logout from /dashboard, user lands on /login instead of / (landing). Protected component's <Navigate to="/login"> fires synchronously when AuthContext clears user, before the inline nav('/') in Navbar runs. Logout itself works (token cleared, navbar shows logged-out state).

action_items: (LOW) Fix logout redirect: in Navbar.jsx, set user to null AFTER navigating, or call nav('/', { replace: true }) before logout(), so that Protected does not race and bounce to /login. Functionally logout works; this is purely UX.
(KNOWN/IGNORE) test_register assertion uses uppercase email — fix the assertion to .lower() to make pytest 15/15 green.
critical_code_review_comments: Landing.jsx provides 6 feature cards and testimonial section as required.
Editor.jsx is large (~420 lines) with all required testids — could split into <Toolbar/>, <LeftRail/>, <RightPanel/> sub-components for maintainability but not blocking.
Editor page does NOT use the global <Navbar/> (uses its own header). Logout in Editor must be done via dashboard — acceptable design choice but worth documenting.

Socket.IO WebSocket upgrade emits one console warning when leaving editor route ('WebSocket is closed before the connection is established'). Polling fallback works. Consider explicit socket.disconnect() in Editor unmount cleanup.
Output panel correctly executes Python via local subprocess (Hello, Coder!), confirming SKIP_PISTON local-fallback path works.
AI endpoints (Gemini 2.5-flash) respond well within ~5-8s; UI shows raw markdown including triple-backticks — could render via react-markdown for prettier output (cosmetic).

success_rate.backend: 93% (14/15, the 1 fail is a known test-file bug per E1)
success_rate.frontend: 100% of critical flows; 1 minor UX issue on logout redirect

test_credentials: alice@test.com / password123 (verified working). New users registered during test prefixed tu* (random uuid).
seed_data_creation: Created 1 new test user (tu0c61a3@test.com / password123) and 1 room ('Test Sprint', 8-char id QCUC1KLX) on alice's account during e2e.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is solid regression suite (auth, rooms, execute py/js/unsupported, 5 AI endpoints, socket.io handshake). Only known issue is test_register's uppercase assertion. Frontend testids are kebab-case across all pages (Landing, Login, Register, Dashboard, Editor, Navbar). Logout from /dashboard races with Protected — lands on /login. AI review returned 1804 chars, AI fix returned code with apply-fix button — both working through Gemini.
rca of the issue: Logout UX bug RCA: Navbar.jsx onClick is `() => { logout(); nav('/'); }`. logout() synchronously updates AuthContext.user=null. React re-renders <Protected> which sees !user and returns <Navigate to='/login' replace/>. This Navigate fires before the imperative nav('/') from useNavigate. Mitigation: either call nav('/', {replace:true}) BEFORE logout(), or put logout()+nav inside a setTimeout(()=>{},0), or move the redirect-on-logout into AuthContext effect targeting '/'.
